role: Co-founder of the Ahmadzadeh-Pouyan group and theorist of armed struggle
bio: Amir Parviz Pouyan (1946–1971) was an Iranian Marxist revolutionary and co-founder of the underground cell known as the Ahmadzadeh-Pouyan group, which merged with [Bijan Jazani](/people/bijan-jazani)'s organisation in 1971 to establish the [Organization of Iranian People's Fadai Guerrillas](/orgs/organization-of-iranian-peoples-fadai-guerrillas). Alongside [Massoud Ahmadzadeh](/people/massoud-ahmadzadeh), Pouyan developed the theoretical justification for armed struggle against the Shah's regime. His essay *The Necessity of Armed Struggle and the Refutation of the Theory of Survival* (1970) became one of the foundational texts of the Iranian guerrilla left, arguing that passive politics was both morally and strategically bankrupt under a dictatorship. Pouyan participated in the [Siahkal incident](/events/siahkal-incident-1971) and was killed in a confrontation with [SAVAK](/orgs/savak) forces in May 1971, just months after the founding operation, becoming one of the movement's first martyrs.
editorial_notes: Co-founder of Ahmadzadeh-Pouyan group. Killed in clash with SAVAK 1971.
